channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Warbond Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Helldivers 2’s next premium Warbond is called Entrenched Division and will arrive on March 17, 2026.

Arrowhead Game Studios just released a major update for Helldivers 2, with the Machinery of Oppression update taking the battle to Cyberstan and introducing 3 new enemy types. Just a month later, the developers have revealed a new Premium Warbond, with fresh weapons, stratagems, armor sets, and more.

Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Weapons and Stratagems

Getting straight into business, Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Premium Warbond comes with a brand-new flamethrower, the B/FLAM-80 Cremator. It is a heavy support weapon that can spray a continuous column of flame thanks to its massive backpack fuel tank. Keeping the flame theme going, there’s also the SMG/FLAM-34 Stoker, a primary submachine gun with iron sights and an incineration attachment.

Then you have the A/GM-17 Gas Mortar Sentry, which is an automated turret that can fire gas shells. Players also receive the P-69 Veto secondary pistol, which comes with jet-assisted rounds. There’s also a new throwable, the G-48 Giga Grenade, which is a good choice when you are going for impact instead of range. Lastly, the CQC-73 Entrenchment Tool will be available as a secondary weapon.

Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Cosmetics

Coming to cosmetics, Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Premium Warbond adds some interesting Armours and Capes along with new emote patterns and more. The first armor is the CPG-48 Sapper, a medium armor that features several pouches and big pockets to store battlefield essentials. It also features Concussive Padding for explosion resistance and room for extra throwables. The Dignity of Toil cape will match perfectly with this armor.

Next up is the light armour, CPH-26 Commandant, which comes with its own Concussive Padding for explosive and gas damage resistance, along with reduced sidearm recoil. This armour set goes perfectly with the new Crest of the Unsullied cape and player card.

Regarding patterns in the Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Premium Warbond, players get the Heritage Olive pattern with the Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Warbond, which can be used on hellpods, FRV, Pelican, and exosuits. The new emote is called Diplomatic Handshake, allowing players to shake on it in the middle of the battlefield.

The details for the new Premium Warbond were revealed through PlayStation’s blog post.

In other news, the release date for Helldivers 2 movie has been revealed. Additionally, the game has reportedly sold more than 20 million copies. What are your thoughts on the Helldivers 2 Entrenched Division Premium Warbond? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Fortnite increasing the price of V-Bucks this month. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fortnite V-Bucks Prices Will Increase This Month
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite has announced that the prices of V-Bucks and Battle Passes will change on March 19, 2026.

The in-game currency used to make purchases in Fortnite for cosmetics and battle passes is called V-Bucks, and the developers have been tinkering with it recently. A few months ago, they announced that players would be able to purchase the ‘exact amount’ of V-Bucks they want. Now, they have changed the pricing structure for the in-game currency.

Fortnite Raises V-Bucks Prices and Drops Battle Pass Prices

As revealed in a new blog post, the V-Bucks price increase comes as “the cost of running Fortnite has gone up a lot”, and the devs are “raising prices to help pay the bills.” Starting March 19, 2026, the $8.99 pack will provide players with 800 V-Bucks instead of 1,000, and will also earn $1.79 back with Epic Rewards. The $22.99 pack will provide 2,400 V-Bucks (previously 2,800 V-Bucks) with a $4.59 return through Epic Rewards.

Coming to the $36.99 pack, which provides 4,500 V-Bucks (previously 5,000 V-Bucks) and returns $7.39 through Epic Rewards. The $89.99 pack now provides 12,500 V-Bucks instead of 13,500 V-Bucks, and players can get get $17.99 back through Epic Rewards. Lastly, the price of the Exact Amount pack has also been increased from $0.50 for 50 V-Bucks to $0.99 for 50 V-Bucks.

Regarding the pricing changes for Fortnite Battle Passes, the main Battle Pass will now cost 800 V-Bucks and grant 800 V-Bucks upon completion. Originally, it was priced at 1,000 V-Bucks and rewarded players with 1,000 V-Bucks plus 500 in the Bonus Rewards. The OG Battle Pass price has been decreased from 1000 V-Bucks to 800 V-Bucks, the Music Pass costs 1,200 V-Bucks instead of 1,400 V-Bucks, and the LEGO Pass price has also been decreased from 1,400 V-Bucks to 1,200 V-Bucks.

Lastly, Fortnite Crew will grant 800 V-Bucks instead of 1,000; the Battle Bundle Cost has been lowered from 2,800 to 2,600 V-Bucks, and existing V-Bucks gift cards will still redeem at their printed values.

In other news, Epic Games has teased another Fortnite and Cyberpunk 2077 crossover. Additionally, a recent claim states that a Game of Thrones collaboration is in development and is ‘coming soon’. What are your thoughts on Fortnite V-Bucks price increases? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the GTA 6 hacker suggesting that the game’s source code is out in the wild. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
